title: Diverse CatBoost plus AutoGluon ensemble
summary: The best runs rely mainly on AutoGluon GBM/XGB with feature tweaks.
rationale: The best runs rely mainly on AutoGluon GBM/XGB with feature tweaks. Adjacent F1 strategy projects report XGBoost success, while this environment also has CatBoost, which often handles categorical interactions differently and can diversify predictions for AUC ensembling.
implementation_hint: Train a standalone CatBoostClassifier with categorical columns Driver, Race, Compound, Year, Stint and the strongest engineered features; use class_weights or scale_pos_weight if positives are rare. Blend CatBoost and AutoGluon probabilities by OOF AUC-optimized weights or simple rank averaging.
expected_effect: May improve public AUC through model diversity and better native categorical handling, even if each individual model is close to the current baseline.
risk: Extra model time may exceed budget; probability calibration is less important for AUC than rank, so rank averaging should be tested against raw averaging.
